featured_analysis.title: The Displacement Continuum
featured_analysis.description: A conceptual framework for understanding how responsibility for displaced populations shifts across legal categories, institutions, territories, and time horizons. Drawing on refugee, IDP, migration, climate, and development contexts, the framework explores where governance continuity breaks down and how responsibility is redistributed over time.
featured_analysis.short_description: A conceptual framework for understanding how responsibility for displaced populations shifts across legal categories, institutions, territories, and time horizons.
featured_analysis.author: Samuel Cheung

featured_analysis.title: Fiscal Architecture of Displacement
featured_analysis.description: An emerging framework for examining how displacement is financed through humanitarian assistance, public budgets, social protection systems, and development finance. The analysis explores how responsibility for displacement is funded, transferred, and sustained as displacement becomes a longer-term public policy challenge.
featured_analysis.short_description: An emerging framework for examining how displacement is financed through humanitarian assistance, public budgets, social protection systems, and development finance.
featured_analysis.author: Samuel Cheung

publications.title: Governing Displacement: Institutional Fault Lines and Sustainable Inclusion
publications.abstract: Research on how institutional arrangements shape the transition from short-term response to sustainable inclusion.
publications.author: Samuel Cheung

publications.title: Social Protection and Displacement
publications.abstract: Examines the role of national protection systems in supporting displaced populations and host communities.
publications.author: Samuel Cheung

publications.title: Development-Induced Displacement and State Absorption Capacity
publications.abstract: Explores how state systems absorb displacement pressures generated by development, infrastructure, and mobility transitions.
publications.author: Samuel Cheung

publications.title: Fiscal Architecture and the Recurrent Obligation Problem
publications.abstract: Considers how public finance systems can account for the sustained costs of displacement inclusion.
publications.author: Samuel Cheung

perspectives.title: From Protection Leadership to Governance Leadership
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: Reflecting on how protection leadership in humanitarian crises increasingly points toward questions of governance and national systems.

perspectives.title: Beyond the IASC Protection Policy
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: Reflections on protection, accountability, and the institutional responsibility required to carry policy commitments in practice.

perspectives.title: Internal Displacement and the Governance Challenge
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: Lessons from global policy development on fragmented responsibility, institutional ownership, financing, and pathways to solutions.

perspectives.title: Durable Solutions and Institutional Inclusion
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: Why durable solutions depend not only on where people live, but whether institutions and systems include them in everyday life.

perspectives.title: The Missing Fiscal Architecture of Displacement
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: How budgets, social protection systems, development finance, and public expenditure shape displacement outcomes.

perspectives.title: Why Displacement Governance Matters
perspectives.author: Samuel Cheung
perspectives.summary: From humanitarian response to public obligation: how responsibility is organised, financed, and sustained over time.
